entries.value: Target Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) is >85%. World-class is >90%. OEE = Availability x Performance x Quality. Availability: actual run time / planned time. Performance: actual output / theoretical max. Quality: good units / total units. Below 60% OEE is critical.

Target defect rate is <1% of total units produced. For precision parts, target <0.5%. Track defects by type (dimensional, cosmetic, functional) and by production line. Defect rate above 2% triggers production hold and root cause analysis.

Target first pass yield (FPY) is >95%. FPY measures the percentage of units that pass quality inspection on the first attempt without rework. Below 90% signals process instability. Track by product and machine.

Monitor actual cycle time vs standard cycle time for each product. Variance >10% indicates equipment degradation, material issues, or operator training gaps. Cycle time creep is often the first sign of maintenance needs.

Target scrap rate is <3% by weight for plastics manufacturing. For injection molding, sprues and runners should be recycled (regrind). Virgin-to-regrind ratio should not exceed 80:20. Track scrap cost monthly.

Target machine uptime is >92% during scheduled production hours. Unplanned downtime should be <3% of total scheduled time. Track downtime by cause: mechanical failure, material shortage, changeover, operator error, planned maintenance.

Alert 30 days before raw material expiration. Critical alert at 7 days. Expired materials must not enter production — quarantine immediately. FIFO (first in, first out) inventory rotation is mandatory for all date-sensitive materials.

Preventive maintenance alerts: 7 days before scheduled date. Critical if overdue by >3 days. Equipment running past maintenance interval has 3x higher unplanned failure rate. Never skip PM to meet production targets.

Apply SMED (Single Minute Exchange of Die) principles: separate internal setup (machine stopped) from external setup (prep while running). Target: all changeovers under 10 minutes. Document every changeover with time study.

Every production run must record raw material lot numbers consumed. In case of a quality escape, lot traceability enables targeted recall of affected finished goods. Minimum retention: 3 years for lot records, 7 years for safety-critical products.
